candidates.stmt:
I am proud to be standing as the Reform UK candidate for The Avenue ward in Fareham.
I have lived locally in Stubbington, Titchfield and Fareham for the past 11 years, and I care deeply about our community and the issues that affect residents every day.
For the last 9 years, I have run my own business, helping people find work and supporting local employers. Earlier in my career, I worked as both a driving instructor and a bus driver, giving me a real understanding of the challenges people face on our roads and in everyday life.
I am standing for Reform UK because I believe local people deserve better representation, more transparency, and real value for money from the council. I am currently supporting the Reform candidate in Fareham Park in opposing the proposed closure of Henry Cort School, which I believe is an important part of our local community.
My priorities for The Avenue ward include:
Improving our roads and tackling ongoing traffic and maintenance issues
Addressing crime and helping residents feel safer in their neighbourhoods
Ensuring council spending is responsible, transparent, and focused on local needs
Delivering better, more reliable local services for residents
I believe in common sense, accountability, and standing up for local people. If elected, I will work hard to ensure residents’ voices are heard and that The Avenue ward gets the attention and investment it deserves.

I’m Kat Bull, the candidate for Avenue Ward and Fareham Titchfield Division. I run a bar on Fareham’s West Street (West Street Alehouse) and have seen the effects a strong community can have on a group of people.

I have owned the Alehouse with my husband since it opened 7 years ago putting us at the heart of Fareham’s Community and Independent Shopping Scene. And I have lived here since 2020, and before that I grew up in Gosport so I am a local gal with local focus!

Some of the things I care deeply about are youth services and schemes to get young people supported and given the chance to do something positive for themselves and their communities.
I also strongly stand behind fair and affordable housing for all. As a long time-renter with small children I know how scary and difficult that can be while also being the only option for many.
I believe if the community is at the heart and focus of everything we do, we can’t fail. When people support and uplift each other everyone thrives. I truly believe I am a lifter.

If you want changes made in Fareham, then it’s time to change those who make all the decisions for us at FBC. I would love to be elected to give the people of Fareham their voice back and speak for you all!

Fareham needs new blood with new ideas. People of Fareham matter, so let’s work together to make our town the best.I am passionate about the area (Highlands) I grew up in and have lived in for more than half of my life. I spent many years struggling as a single parent! This is why I created a Facebook group called ‘Penny Pinchers’, to share ideas and advice on how to save the pennies. I am now married to Sean and we now have a eight-year-old together. Between us both, we have six grandchildren.

If elected, I would work hard on your behalf and will NOT fill your heads with empty promises I cannot deliver on. What you see is what you get, no hidden agenda's and total transparency.

SOME OF WHAT I’VE BEEN UP TO:

Sovereign Patients Forum: I was invited to join as admin on 'Sovereign Patients Forum on Facebook , because of my passionate nature to help others. Many people were experiencing some serious issues with the surgeries and although I am not a patient there, my family are . So we are aiming for a better service from our GP's, but this is still a work in progress.

Dangers Outside School: I am currently looking at raising awareness of the dangers outside school gates, to the children and local residents. I have witnessed myself first hand how dangerous these areas are, by nearly being knocked over by a parent driving at speed around a blind corner, because another vehicle was blocking both our vision, parked on the corner. I cannot promise I will be successful, but I will certainly give it my all and hopefully save someone's life.

I grew up in Fareham, attended Uplands Primary School, Cams Hill and St Vincent’s College, and this a community that has shaped my values and who I am today.

I believe that social fairness and protecting our environment are the foundations of a strong, successful community, and that the opportunities I had shouldn’t be taken away from others. This is why I oppose austerity policies that push people into hardship, while failing to benefit society as a whole.

I have held senior positions in some of the world’s largest multinational organisations, developing strong leadership and budgeting skills that I hope to put to work for Fareham’s residents.I feel honoured to have the opportunity to represent the town that means so much to me.

Andrew has lived in Fareham for 26 years and currently works for a large local employer.

He has a particular interest in fitness, health and wellbeing, and undertakes a number of voluntary and charity raising activities each year.

A keen cyclist, Andrew is concerned about the availability of affordable and social housing in Fareham, the importance of preserving green spaces against over development and the need to provide alternative environmentally friendly transport options within Fareham.

My name is Kirsten Wiltshire, I’m 47 years old, married with 3 children and I’ve lived in and loved Fareham since I was 10 years old. I’ve only recently got into politics when I discovered my naïve view that the local council had our best interests at heart was a huge misjudgement! I feel that we no longer have a voice and my view is that the local areas should be run by the people who actually live there taking politics out of the equation. All residents should be listened to and heard, where change is needed it should be a collective decision by the residents directly affected. I have decided to stand as the Warsash Councillor for the Fareham Independent Group in the upcoming election and if you feel that your voice needs to be heard then vote for me & I will do everything in my power to make the changes necessary.

* Fighting for building genuinely affordable houses on brownfield sites and keeping the green spaces! * Lobby on local issues for local people - giving you your voice back! * Investigating transparency within the council ensuring accountability on what our local taxes get spent on!

I was born in Portsea and grew up in Leigh Park, where I experienced first-hand the strength of community spirit and the importance of looking out for one another. I moved to Fareham in 1987 and, over the past 39 years, have seen how our town has changed and grown.
Throughout my working life, I have been dedicated to caring for others, spending many years in both the private care sector and the NHS before retiring. This experience has given me a deep understanding of the challenges facing our health and care services, as well as the importance of compassion, reliability, and community support.
I have also worked as company secretary for my husband’s small business, where I saw the realities of running a business in today’s challenging economic climate. This has given me a strong appreciation for the pressures faced by local businesses and the vital role they play in our community.
As a mother of three and grandmother of seven, I care deeply about the future of our community. I am passionate about improving wellbeing, safety, education, and opportunities for the next generation.
I am proud to be standing as a Reform UK candidate for the Fareham Town ward. My priorities are to address crime, help residents feel safer in their neighbourhoods, and ensure the delivery of better, more reliable local services for everyone.

Vote for Stephen Ingram as your hard-working, local Councillor to maintain great services in Fareham and keep your Council tax low. Stephen has a particular interest in reducing crime, drug taking and anti-social behavior that's sadly creeping into our pleasant town.

INTRODUCTION TO CANDIDATE 

Hi, I'm Dominic Martin, I am 22 years old and I am running to be your local councillor in the upcoming local elections on May 5th. I am a graduate in International Relations and Politics from Portsmouth University,  a former local barista and now a telecommunications worker in the town centre.

I have been involved in local politics since I was 14 years old, that's 8 years of listening to local issues and campaigning for a better Portchester, where I have lived all my life. It all started with a campaign to keep school crossing patrols in place. 

I came into politics due to family circumstances and observations of our local community; where I realised that if you want to get things done, if you want to change your community for the better, then politics combined with community action through community groups is how it's done. Now I want to put that into practice by being your local councillor.

Graham Everdell has lived with his family in Park Gate since 2003 and is standing for Fareham Council’s Park Gate seat. With his three sons at university and college, and now retired from a career in financial services, Graham is seeking the opportunity to positively influence how Fareham Council serves all residents of the Borough.

My name is David (Dave) Wiltshire. I am aged 48, married with 3 children, all of whom attended Portchester Primary and Secondary Schools. I was resident in Portchester for 20+ years, during this time I was married in our wonderful St Mary’s Church at Portchester Castle.

I have very strong ties with the village of Portchester. I have been a volunteer at The Box Youth Club since it was created over 3 years ago. I feel that our children need a safe place to meet and socialise after school.

I am heavily involved with Conserving Habitats in Portchester (CHIP). I believe we need to ensure that any new developments are situated on re-purposed brownfield sites in preference to building on our green spaces and that all the right infrastructure is in place to support them.

I founded and ran a martial arts group based in Portchester for children and adults for over 10 years.

I was a Special Constable for Hampshire Constabulary for over 12 years.

I would like to be given the opportunity to listen to the residents of Portchester and fight for their concerns, thereby giving them a voice. I believe a change needs to happen and with your support we can all be part of that change.

I am standing as the Conservative candidate for
Sarisbury and Whiteley in the local elections on
7th May. I have lived in Whiteley for 30 years,
raising my two sons there, both of whom attended
Whiteley Primary School.
A vacancy this year prompted my decision to
stand, determined to continue the incredibly strong
record of Sarisbury Conservative Councillors and
give back to the community in which I am proud to
live. I have long supported local activities,
delivering the InTouch newsletters and working to
secure the reopening of the popular pathway
linking Meadowside Leisure Centre, Whiteley to
the cinema, restaurants and Tesco.
I am a Governor at Sarisbury Junior School, a
Trustee at Whiteley Community Centre and
enjoy gardening, fitness and canoeing. I am
committed to working with Councillor Joanne Burton to provide dedicated, reliable representation for local people.
I very much hope you will vote for me to enable
me to serve you to the best of my ability.

Gayathri Sathyanath is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Havant. A proud first-generation immigrant from India, she has studied Environmental Science and runs her own business.

Gayathri cannot stand by and watch our rivers and seas being flooded with excrement. She will hold the water companies accountable for damaging the environment.

Gayathri’s husband and son are doctors in the local area, so she understands how the NHS has crumbled under 14 years of Tory rule. She will fight for a fair deal for the NHS to reduce waiting lists, guarantee quick treatment for cancer patients, increase GP numbers, and appoint a mental health professional in each and every school.

She wants to ease the cost-of-living crisis by implementing windfall taxes on energy companies, doubling the winter fuel allowance, and providing a warm home discount in winter months.

She says "We need change nationally and locally, with a new government and new leadership that puts our community first."

Polly, who runs her own local accountancy business and is a proud mum to two little boys, has lived in Fareham and the surrounding Hampshire countryside most of her life. Taking into account recent global crises, Polly strongly believes there has never been a better time to vote Green and grow our own British energy, for the sake of our children's future and our budgets! She has no patience for wasted taxpayer money and her No1 priority is making the cost of living easier on her neighbours by ensuring you get maximum value for money. Having worked for several years in central government managing multi £million budgets, Polly has the financial experience and credentials to back this up!

Kasi is a Portsmouth native, but has lived in Fareham since 2012, where she has been raising her family with her husband. She is passionate about social justice and taking care of the environment, regularly doing litter picks on the beach with her family.

Ciaran Urry-Tuttiett has lived in Portchester/Fareham his whole life and now lives in Funtley. Ciaran is deeply committed to local communities and will work to keep our places safe, clean, and friendly.
